title: DOT cranks up its campaign to strip bike lane references from federal websites
excerpt: WASHINGTON — In the two weeks since Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy vowed to end support for "DEI bike lanes," a growing list of bike-related safety materials have disappeared from the Federal Highway Administration's website.
content_html:
WASHINGTON — In the two weeks since Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy vowed to end support for "DEI bike lanes," a growing list of bike-related safety materials have disappeared from the Federal Highway Administration's website.

Even agency staff have been left guessing about why, said a person who works in web support for agencies at the Department of Transportation. They requested anonymity because they fear retribution for speaking publicly.

The person said FHWA staff have been asked to find, and in some cases take down, any references to bikes and bike lanes.

Related Story: NPR

"Why do you need to take down bicycle safety, of all things?" they told NPR.

"We're not making the sites better, faster or quicker, you know, able to provide real information to the communities that use these resources," they said. "It's asinine."

Questions remain over Trump administration's review of safety resources

Federal transportation officials have pulled down or edited dozens of pages of safety resources in the past week, according to an analysis by the Internet Archive Wayback Machine and internal FHWA emails obtained by NPR.

While there has been no official explanation, the changes appear to be part of a broader push to find and remove road safety materials from the FHWA website.

Earlier this month, the FHWA quietly removed a set of recommendations — including bike lanes and speed cameras — from a list of "Proven Safety Countermeasures" that have been vetted by agency staff and shown to improve road safety.

A DOT spokesperson told NPR in an emailed statement on Wednesday that the department "continuously reviews its policies to ensure each promotes safety, efficiency, and common sense."

"Since nearly 80% of the population uses cars to get to work and their important medical appointments, we are taking a closer look at federal guidance to ensure American families have the infrastructure they need," the spokesperson said. "This is a common sense approach despite the Left's belief that everyone should ride a bike to their shoebox-sized apartment to eat crickets to achieve a phony climate agenda."

But safety advocates warn that the Trump administration is undermining proven measures for reducing roadway crashes and fatalities, including some that date back decades.

One of the safety tools that has been taken down is known as the Pedestrian and Bicycle Crash Analysis Tool, or PBCAT. As of Thursday, a link to the page that formerly hosted the tool on the FHWA website was dead.

The tool is designed to capture detailed information about crashes involving cars, bicycles and pedestrians, including what combinations of vehicle maneuvers and roadway conditions led to the crash, which can help planners and safety advocates develop countermeasures to prevent similar crashes.

"That's really important because it helps us do a better job of tracking pedestrian and bicyclist fatalities across the nation," said David Harkey, who was the lead researcher on the project that initially developed the tool at the University of North Carolina's Highway Safety Research Center.

Today, Harkey is the president of the Insurance Institute for Highway Safety (IIHS), a nonprofit organization funded by the auto insurance industry that works to reduce motor vehicle crash fatalities and injuries.

Harkey told NPR he's disappointed by the decision to remove the tool from the FHWA's website.

"This is an agency that claims that safety is a priority for them," he said. "It's important that proven tools and resources like PBCAT are there on their site and are available for safety professionals across the country."

Local and state officials may still be able to make use of PBCAT even if it's not publicly available on the FHWA website, Harkey said, because it's already embedded in the way they track crashes. Still, he would prefer the tool to be widely available from FHWA.

Based on his organization's conversations with FHWA staff, Harkey says the web tool remains "under review," though he said the details are murky.

"It's not clear to us what is being reviewed, what the criteria are, what they are looking for," Harkey said. "Hopefully this review will get completed sooner rather than later."

But Harkey isn't optimistic. He says the FHWA pulled down another critical safety tool — a database known as the Highway Safety Information System — 18 months ago. It still hasn't been restored.

"That's one of the frightening things about this process," Harkey said. "We've heard the same story that things are under review, and that once that review is completed, they hope to be able to put it back up. But we haven't seen that."

DOT staff are told to remove bike safety language 2 days after Duffy's "DEI" post 

Neither the FHWA nor the Transportation Department responded to questions about the criteria for the review, or how long it would take.

But an FHWA internal email seen by NPR provides a clearer picture of when the removal of bike- and safety-related materials from the FWHA website began.

Earlier this month, DOT announced more than $1.7 billion in discretionary grants that did not include funding for bike lanes or pedestrian projects. The Biden administration, by contrast, had used the same program to fund hundreds of millions of dollars in bike lanes and trails nationwide, along with other road and infrastructure projects.

"We're proud to support these great projects that will improve infrastructure, strengthen supply chains, make us safer, advance equity, and combat climate change," said then-Transportation Secretary Pete Buttigieg when he announced the grants in 2021.

Secretary Duffy said in a July 7 post on X that the Biden administration "used YOUR MONEY for DEI bike lanes and climate change. THAT'S OVER."

In response, critics noted that FHWA had previously acknowledged that bicycle lanes can make roads safer for all users.

But on July 9, a staffer in the FHWA's safety office sent an email with the subject line, "URGENT ACTION: Update Website," directing staff to remove that language from the website.

Specifically, the email directed staff to make significant changes to the list of "Proven Safety Countermeasures," as they're known, that have been shown to reduce crashes and save lives. "We received an urgent request from leadership to update web content in alignment with administration direction," the sender wrote, requesting that references to bike lanes, speed cameras and several other strategies related to speed be stripped from the list.

Safety advocates say it's normal for funding priorities to shift from one administration to another. But many say they're troubled by the push to remove evidence-based safety measures from DOT websites.

Related Story: NPR

Related Story: NPR

The person who works in web support for DOT agencies shares that concern.

"OK, got it. No more grants," they said. "But why does that mean I need to take down content? Like, Pedestrian Bicycle Crash Analysis Tool — who did it ever hurt, you know?"

More than 36,000 people were killed on U.S. roads last year. That number has been trending down since 2021, and the crash fatality rate in the U.S. is near an all-time low. Still, crash and fatality rates here remain far higher than in other developed countries.

The 2025 total includes more than 6,700 pedestrians and bicyclists who were killed.

"Safety involves everyone on the roadway," said Harkey, the IIHS president. "So if you really, truly believe as an agency that your job is to make sure safety is a priority, then you cannot remove tools and resources that are there to help you address those 20% of the fatalities on our roadways."

Transcript

MICHEL MARTIN, HOST:

Trump's Department of Transportation is trying to put the brakes on bike lanes. Earlier this month, Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy attacked what he called - and I'm quoting here - "DEI bike lanes." Now transportation officials are removing safety materials that mention bikes from federal websites. NPR's Joel Rose has new reporting on this today, and he's with us now. Good morning, Joel.

JOEL ROSE, BYLINE: Hey, Michel.

MARTIN: OK. Can we just start with - what is a DEI bike lane?

ROSE: That is a phrase that Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy used in a social media post earlier this month. Duffy was announcing $1.7 billion in federal grants, which did not include any money for bike or pedestrian projects. And Duffy was trying to draw a contrast with the Biden administration, which had used the same grant program to fund hundreds of millions of dollars in bike lanes and recreational trails and touted the equity benefits of those projects, including the bike lanes.

Some Trump administration critics then noted that the Federal Highway Administration, which is part of DOT, had previously acknowledged that bike lanes make roads safer for everyone. The next day, the web support staff at the Federal Highway Administration received an urgent request to take that language down from the website to align with White House policy.

MARTIN: Do we know exactly what got taken down?

ROSE: Yeah. Almost two weeks ago, the DOT trimmed a list of safety measures that have been proven to reduce crashes and save lives. Five of those recommendations were quietly removed from the list, including bike lanes and also speed cameras. And we learned this week that other safety materials have disappeared, too. I talked with a person who works in web support for various DOT agencies. They requested anonymity because they fear retribution for speaking out publicly. They say the staff at the Federal Highway Administration have been directed to look for the words bike and bike lanes in online safety materials. And in the past week, staff have edited or even pulled down dozens of pages.

MARTIN: Can you give us an example?

ROSE: Yeah. One of the pages that was removed in the past week is something called the Pedestrian Bicycle Crash Analysis Tool, which helps researchers and planners understand what happened in crashes involving cars and bikes and pedestrians so that they can prevent similar crashes. I talked to the researcher who originally led the development of this tool more than two decades ago. His name is David Harkey. Today he is the president of the Insurance Institute for Highway Safety, which is the big nonprofit that is funded by the auto insurance industry. Harkey is not happy that this tool is no longer available on the Federal Highway website.

DAVID HARKEY: This is an agency that claims that safety is a priority for them, and safety involves everyone on the roadway. And so we're very disappointed in seeing these kinds of resources removed from the site.

ROSE: Harkey says his organization has been told that this particular tool is, quote, "under review." But he has no details about what the criteria of that review are or how long it might last.

MARTIN: Have we had any explanation of why this is happening?

ROSE: There has been no public explanation either from the Federal Highway Administration or from the Department of Transportation. But we did get an emailed statement from DOT yesterday. A spokesperson says the department continuously reviews its policies to ensure that they promote, quote, "safety, efficiency and common sense," unquote. The statement notes that nearly 80% of the U.S. population uses cars to get to work and says DOT is taking a closer look at federal guidance to ensure that American families have the infrastructure they need.

The spokesperson also touted recent declines in the number of people killed on U.S. roads, which fell to about 36,000 last year. And it is true that the crash fatality rate in the U.S. is near an all-time low. But it still remains far higher than the crash rates in other developed countries - in some cases, double. And nearly 20% of people killed on U.S. roads last year - more than 6,700 - were pedestrians or bicyclists.

MARTIN: That is NPR's Joel Rose. Joel, thank you.

ROSE: You're welcome.

(SOUNDBITE OF MUSIC)

sentences.zh: 北卡罗来纳大学公路安全研究中心最初开发该工具的项目首席研究员戴维·哈基 (David Harkey) 表示：“这非常重要，因为它可以帮助我们更好地追踪全国范围内的行人和骑自行车者的死亡情况。”

blocks.html_en: Today, Harkey is the president of the Insurance Institute for Highway Safety (IIHS), a nonprofit organization funded by the auto insurance industry that works to reduce motor vehicle crash fatalities and injuries.

sentences.en: Today, Harkey is the president of the Insurance Institute for Highway Safety (IIHS), a nonprofit organization funded by the auto insurance industry that works to reduce motor vehicle crash fatalities and injuries.
sentences.zh: 如今，哈基是公路安全保险协会 (IIHS) 的主席，这是一个由汽车保险行业资助的非营利组织，致力于减少机动车碰撞伤亡。

blocks.html_en: Harkey told NPR he's disappointed by the decision to remove the tool from the FHWA's website.

sentences.en: Harkey told NPR he's disappointed by the decision to remove the tool from the FHWA's website.
sentences.zh: 哈基告诉 NPR，他对从 FHWA 网站上删除该工具的决定感到失望。

blocks.html_en: "This is an agency that claims that safety is a priority for them," he said. "It's important that proven tools and resources like PBCAT are there on their site and are available for safety professionals across the country."

sentences.en: "This is an agency that claims that safety is a priority for them," he said.
sentences.zh: “这个机构声称安全是他们的首要任务，”他说。

sentences.en: "It's important that proven tools and resources like PBCAT are there on their site and are available for safety professionals across the country."
sentences.zh: “重要的是，他们的网站上有像 PBCAT 这样经过验证的工具和资源，可供全国各地的安全专业人员使用。”

blocks.html_en: Local and state officials may still be able to make use of PBCAT even if it's not publicly available on the FHWA website, Harkey said, because it's already embedded in the way they track crashes. Still, he would prefer the tool to be widely available from FHWA.

sentences.en: Local and state officials may still be able to make use of PBCAT even if it's not publicly available on the FHWA website, Harkey said, because it's already embedded in the way they track crashes.
sentences.zh: 哈基说，即使 PBCAT 未在 FHWA 网站上公开提供，地方和州官员仍可能能够使用它，因为它已经嵌入到他们跟踪事故的方式中。

sentences.en: Still, he would prefer the tool to be widely available from FHWA.
sentences.zh: 尽管如此，他还是希望 FHWA 能够广泛提供该工具。

blocks.html_en: Based on his organization's conversations with FHWA staff, Harkey says the web tool remains "under review," though he said the details are murky.

sentences.en: Based on his organization's conversations with FHWA staff, Harkey says the web tool remains "under review," though he said the details are murky.
sentences.zh: 根据他的组织与 FHWA 工作人员的对话，哈基表示该网络工具仍在“审查中”，但他表示细节尚不清楚。

blocks.html_en: "It's not clear to us what is being reviewed, what the criteria are, what they are looking for," Harkey said. "Hopefully this review will get completed sooner rather than later."

sentences.en: "It's not clear to us what is being reviewed, what the criteria are, what they are looking for," Harkey said.
sentences.zh: 哈基说：“我们不清楚正在审查什么、标准是什么、他们在寻找什么。”

sentences.en: "Hopefully this review will get completed sooner rather than later."
sentences.zh: “希望这次审查能够早日完成。”

blocks.html_en: But Harkey isn't optimistic. He says the FHWA pulled down another critical safety tool — a database known as the Highway Safety Information System — 18 months ago. It still hasn't been restored.

sentences.en: But Harkey isn't optimistic.
sentences.zh: 但哈基并不乐观。

sentences.en: He says the FHWA pulled down another critical safety tool — a database known as the Highway Safety Information System — 18 months ago.
sentences.zh: 他说，FHWA 在 18 个月前撤下了另一个重要的安全工具——一个名为高速公路安全信息系统的数据库。

sentences.en: It still hasn't been restored.
sentences.zh: 它还没有恢复。

blocks.html_en: "That's one of the frightening things about this process," Harkey said. "We've heard the same story that things are under review, and that once that review is completed, they hope to be able to put it back up. But we haven't seen that."

sentences.en: "That's one of the frightening things about this process," Harkey said.
sentences.zh: “这是这个过程中最可怕的事情之一，”哈基说。

sentences.en: "We've heard the same story that things are under review, and that once that review is completed, they hope to be able to put it back up.
sentences.zh: “我们听到了同样的故事，事情正在接受审查，一旦审查完成，他们希望能够将其恢复。

sentences.en: But we haven't seen that."
sentences.zh: 但我们还没有看到这一点。”

blocks.html_en: DOT staff are told to remove bike safety language 2 days after Duffy's "DEI" post

sentences.en: DOT staff are told to remove bike safety language 2 days after Duffy's "DEI" post
sentences.zh: 达菲发布“DEI”帖子两天后，交通部工作人员被告知删除自行车安全用语

blocks.html_en: Neither the FHWA nor the Transportation Department responded to questions about the criteria for the review, or how long it would take.

sentences.en: Neither the FHWA nor the Transportation Department responded to questions about the criteria for the review, or how long it would take.
sentences.zh: 美国联邦公路管理局和交通运输部都没有回答有关审查标准或需要多长时间的问题。

blocks.html_en: But an FHWA internal email seen by NPR provides a clearer picture of when the removal of bike- and safety-related materials from the FWHA website began.

sentences.en: But an FHWA internal email seen by NPR provides a clearer picture of when the removal of bike- and safety-related materials from the FWHA website began.
sentences.zh: 但 NPR 看到的一封 FHWA 内部电子邮件提供了更清晰的信息，显示 FWHA 网站何时开始删除与自行车和安全相关的材料。

blocks.html_en: Earlier this month, DOT announced more than $1.7 billion in discretionary grants that did not include funding for bike lanes or pedestrian projects. The Biden administration, by contrast, had used the same program to fund hundreds of millions of dollars in bike lanes and trails nationwide, along with other road and infrastructure projects.

sentences.en: Earlier this month, DOT announced more than $1.7 billion in discretionary grants that did not include funding for bike lanes or pedestrian projects.
sentences.zh: 本月早些时候，交通部宣布提供超过 17 亿美元的酌情拨款，其中不包括自行车道或行人项目的资金。

sentences.en: The Biden administration, by contrast, had used the same program to fund hundreds of millions of dollars in bike lanes and trails nationwide, along with other road and infrastructure projects.
sentences.zh: 相比之下，拜登政府利用同一计划为全国自行车道和小径以及其他道路和基础设施项目提供了数亿美元的资金。

blocks.html_en: "We're proud to support these great projects that will improve infrastructure, strengthen supply chains, make us safer, advance equity, and combat climate change," said then-Transportation Secretary Pete Buttigieg when he announced the grants in 2021.

sentences.en: "We're proud to support these great projects that will improve infrastructure, strengthen supply chains, make us safer, advance equity, and combat climate change," said then-Transportation Secretary Pete Buttigieg when he announced the grants in 2021.
sentences.zh: 时任交通部长皮特·布蒂吉格 (Pete Buttigieg) 在 2021 年宣布拨款时表示：“我们很自豪能够支持这些伟大的项目，这些项目将改善基础设施、加强供应链、让我们更安全、促进公平并应对气候变化。”

blocks.html_en: Secretary Duffy said in a July 7 post on X that the Biden administration "used YOUR MONEY for DEI bike lanes and climate change. THAT'S OVER."

sentences.en: Secretary Duffy said in a July 7 post on X that the Biden administration "used YOUR MONEY for DEI bike lanes and climate change.
sentences.zh: 国务卿达菲在 7 月 7 日的 X 帖子中表示，拜登政府“将你们的钱用于 DEI 自行车道和气候变化。

sentences.en: THAT'S OVER."
sentences.zh: 结束了。”

blocks.html_en: In response, critics noted that FHWA had previously acknowledged that bicycle lanes can make roads safer for all users.

sentences.en: In response, critics noted that FHWA had previously acknowledged that bicycle lanes can make roads safer for all users.
sentences.zh: 对此，批评者指出，联邦公路管理局此前曾承认，自行车道可以使道路对所有用户来说更安全。

blocks.html_en: But on July 9, a staffer in the FHWA's safety office sent an email with the subject line, "URGENT ACTION: Update Website," directing staff to remove that language from the website.

sentences.en: But on July 9, a staffer in the FHWA's safety office sent an email with the subject line, "URGENT ACTION: Update Website," directing staff to remove that language from the website.
sentences.zh: 但 7 月 9 日，FHWA 安全办公室的一名工作人员发送了一封主题为“紧急行动：更新网站”的电子邮件，指示工作人员从网站上删除该语言。

blocks.html_en: Specifically, the email directed staff to make significant changes to the list of "Proven Safety Countermeasures," as they're known, that have been shown to reduce crashes and save lives. "We received an urgent request from leadership to update web content in alignment with administration direction," the sender wrote, requesting that references to bike lanes, speed cameras and several other strategies related to speed be stripped from the list.

sentences.en: Specifically, the email directed staff to make significant changes to the list of "Proven Safety Countermeasures," as they're known, that have been shown to reduce crashes and save lives.
sentences.zh: 具体来说，这封电子邮件指示工作人员对“经过验证的安全对策”列表进行重大更改，正如他们所知，这些措施已被证明可以减少事故并挽救生命。

sentences.en: "We received an urgent request from leadership to update web content in alignment with administration direction," the sender wrote, requesting that references to bike lanes, speed cameras and several other strategies related to speed be stripped from the list.
sentences.zh: 发件人写道：“我们收到了领导层的紧急要求，要求根据管理方向更新网络内容。”他要求将自行车道、测速摄像头和其他与速度相关的策略从列表中删除。

blocks.html_en: Safety advocates say it's normal for funding priorities to shift from one administration to another. But many say they're troubled by the push to remove evidence-based safety measures from DOT websites.

sentences.en: Safety advocates say it's normal for funding priorities to shift from one administration to another.
sentences.zh: 安全倡导者表示，资助优先事项从一届政府转移到另一届政府是正常的。

sentences.en: But many say they're troubled by the push to remove evidence-based safety measures from DOT websites.
sentences.zh: 但许多人表示，他们对交通部网站上取消基于证据的安全措施的推动感到不安。

blocks.html_en: Related Story: NPR

sentences.en: Related Story: NPR
sentences.zh: 相关报道：NPR

blocks.html_en: Related Story: NPR

sentences.en: Related Story: NPR
sentences.zh: 相关报道：NPR

blocks.html_en: The person who works in web support for DOT agencies shares that concern.

sentences.en: The person who works in web support for DOT agencies shares that concern.
sentences.zh: 为 DOT 机构提供网络支持的人员也有同样的担忧。

blocks.html_en: "OK, got it. No more grants," they said. "But why does that mean I need to take down content? Like, Pedestrian Bicycle Crash Analysis Tool — who did it ever hurt, you know?"

sentences.en: "OK, got it.
sentences.zh: “好的，明白了。

sentences.en: No more grants," they said.
sentences.zh: 不再有补助金，”他们说。

sentences.en: "But why does that mean I need to take down content?
sentences.zh: “但这为什么意味着我需要删除内容呢？

sentences.en: Like, Pedestrian Bicycle Crash Analysis Tool — who did it ever hurt, you know?"
sentences.zh: 比如，步行自行车碰撞分析工具——它伤害了谁，你知道吗？”

blocks.html_en: More than 36,000 people were killed on U.S. roads last year. That number has been trending down since 2021, and the crash fatality rate in the U.S. is near an all-time low. Still, crash and fatality rates here remain far higher than in other developed countries.

sentences.en: More than 36,000 people were killed on U.S. roads last year.
sentences.zh: 去年，超过 36,000 人在美国道路上丧生。

sentences.en: That number has been trending down since 2021, and the crash fatality rate in the U.S. is near an all-time low.
sentences.zh: 自 2021 年以来，这一数字一直呈下降趋势，美国的车祸死亡率接近历史最低点。

sentences.en: Still, crash and fatality rates here remain far higher than in other developed countries.
sentences.zh: 尽管如此，这里的车祸率和死亡率仍然远高于其他发达国家。

blocks.html_en: The 2025 total includes more than 6,700 pedestrians and bicyclists who were killed.

sentences.en: The 2025 total includes more than 6,700 pedestrians and bicyclists who were killed.
sentences.zh: 到 2025 年，死亡总数包括超过 6,700 名行人和骑自行车的人。

blocks.html_en: "Safety involves everyone on the roadway," said Harkey, the IIHS president. "So if you really, truly believe as an agency that your job is to make sure safety is a priority, then you cannot remove tools and resources that are there to help you address those 20% of the fatalities on our roadways."

sentences.en: "Safety involves everyone on the roadway," said Harkey, the IIHS president.
sentences.zh: “安全涉及道路上的每个人，”IIHS 主席哈基说。

sentences.en: "So if you really, truly believe as an agency that your job is to make sure safety is a priority, then you cannot remove tools and resources that are there to help you address those 20% of the fatalities on our roadways."
sentences.zh: “因此，作为一个机构，如果你真的相信你的工作是确保安全是首要任务，那么你就无法移除可以帮助你解决道路上 20% 死亡事故的工具和资源。”

blocks.html_en: Transcript

sentences.en: Transcript
sentences.zh: 成绩单

blocks.html_en: MICHEL MARTIN, HOST:

sentences.en: MICHEL MARTIN, HOST:
sentences.zh: 米歇尔·马丁，主持人：

blocks.html_en: Trump's Department of Transportation is trying to put the brakes on bike lanes. Earlier this month, Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy attacked what he called - and I'm quoting here - "DEI bike lanes." Now transportation officials are removing safety materials that mention bikes from federal websites. NPR's Joel Rose has new reporting on this today, and he's with us now. Good morning, Joel.

sentences.en: Trump's Department of Transportation is trying to put the brakes on bike lanes.
sentences.zh: 特朗普的交通部正试图对自行车道实施刹车。

sentences.en: Earlier this month, Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy attacked what he called - and I'm quoting here - "DEI bike lanes."
sentences.zh: 本月早些时候，交通部长肖恩·达菲 (Sean Duffy) 攻击了他所说的——我在这里引用——“DEI 自行车道”。

sentences.en: Now transportation officials are removing safety materials that mention bikes from federal websites.
sentences.zh: 现在，交通官员正在从联邦网站上删除提及自行车的安全材料。

sentences.en: NPR's Joel Rose has new reporting on this today, and he's with us now.
sentences.zh: NPR 的乔尔·罗斯今天对此进行了新报道，他现在就在我们身边。

sentences.en: Good morning, Joel.
sentences.zh: 早上好，乔尔。

blocks.html_en: JOEL ROSE, BYLINE: Hey, Michel.

sentences.en: JOEL ROSE, BYLINE: Hey, Michel.
sentences.zh: 乔尔·罗斯连线：嘿，米歇尔。

blocks.html_en: MARTIN: OK. Can we just start with - what is a DEI bike lane?

sentences.en: MARTIN: OK.
sentences.zh: 马丁：好的。

sentences.en: Can we just start with - what is a DEI bike lane?
sentences.zh: 我们可以从 - 什么是 DEI 自行车道开始吗？

blocks.html_en: ROSE: That is a phrase that Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy used in a social media post earlier this month. Duffy was announcing $1.7 billion in federal grants, which did not include any money for bike or pedestrian projects. And Duffy was trying to draw a contrast with the Biden administration, which had used the same grant program to fund hundreds of millions of dollars in bike lanes and recreational trails and touted the equity benefits of those projects, including the bike lanes.

sentences.en: ROSE: That is a phrase that Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy used in a social media post earlier this month.
sentences.zh: 罗斯：这是运输部长肖恩·达菲本月早些时候在社交媒体帖子中使用的一句话。

sentences.en: Duffy was announcing $1.7 billion in federal grants, which did not include any money for bike or pedestrian projects.
sentences.zh: 达菲宣布提供 17 亿美元的联邦拨款，其中不包括用于自行车或步行项目的任何资金。

sentences.en: And Duffy was trying to draw a contrast with the Biden administration, which had used the same grant program to fund hundreds of millions of dollars in bike lanes and recreational trails and touted the equity benefits of those projects, including the bike lanes.
sentences.zh: 达菲试图与拜登政府形成鲜明对比，拜登政府利用相同的拨款计划为自行车道和休闲步道提供了数亿美元的资金，并宣扬这些项目（包括自行车道）的股权效益。

blocks.html_en: Some Trump administration critics then noted that the Federal Highway Administration, which is part of DOT, had previously acknowledged that bike lanes make roads safer for everyone. The next day, the web support staff at the Federal Highway Administration received an urgent request to take that language down from the website to align with White House policy.

sentences.en: Some Trump administration critics then noted that the Federal Highway Administration, which is part of DOT, had previously acknowledged that bike lanes make roads safer for everyone.
sentences.zh: 一些特朗普政府的批评者随后指出，隶属于交通部的联邦公路管理局此前曾承认，自行车道使道路对每个人来说都更安全。

sentences.en: The next day, the web support staff at the Federal Highway Administration received an urgent request to take that language down from the website to align with White House policy.
sentences.zh: 第二天，联邦公路管理局的网络支持人员收到了一项紧急请求，要求从网站上删除该语言，以符合白宫政策。

blocks.html_en: MARTIN: Do we know exactly what got taken down?

sentences.en: MARTIN: Do we know exactly what got taken down?
sentences.zh: 马丁：我们确切知道什么被删除了吗？

blocks.html_en: ROSE: Yeah. Almost two weeks ago, the DOT trimmed a list of safety measures that have been proven to reduce crashes and save lives. Five of those recommendations were quietly removed from the list, including bike lanes and also speed cameras. And we learned this week that other safety materials have disappeared, too. I talked with a person who works in web support for various DOT agencies. They requested anonymity because they fear retribution for speaking out publicly. They say the staff at the Federal Highway Administration have been directed to look for the words bike and bike lanes in online safety materials. And in the past week, staff have edited or even pulled down dozens of pages.

sentences.en: ROSE: Yeah.
sentences.zh: 罗斯：是的。

sentences.en: Almost two weeks ago, the DOT trimmed a list of safety measures that have been proven to reduce crashes and save lives.
sentences.zh: 大约两周前，交通部削减了一系列已被证明可以减少事故并挽救生命的安全措施。

sentences.en: Five of those recommendations were quietly removed from the list, including bike lanes and also speed cameras.
sentences.zh: 其中五项建议已悄悄从列表中删除，包括自行车道和超速摄像头。

sentences.en: And we learned this week that other safety materials have disappeared, too.
sentences.zh: 我们本周了解到其他安全材料也消失了。

sentences.en: I talked with a person who works in web support for various DOT agencies.
sentences.zh: 我与一位为 DOT 各机构从事网络支持工作的人进行了交谈。

sentences.en: They requested anonymity because they fear retribution for speaking out publicly.
sentences.zh: 他们要求匿名，因为他们担心公开发言会遭到报复。

sentences.en: They say the staff at the Federal Highway Administration have been directed to look for the words bike and bike lanes in online safety materials.
sentences.zh: 他们说，联邦公路管理局的工作人员已被指示在在线安全材料中查找“自行车”和“自行车道”一词。

sentences.en: And in the past week, staff have edited or even pulled down dozens of pages.
sentences.zh: 在过去的一周里，工作人员编辑甚至撤下了数十页内容。

blocks.html_en: MARTIN: Can you give us an example?

sentences.en: MARTIN: Can you give us an example?
sentences.zh: 马丁：你能给我们举个例子吗？

blocks.html_en: ROSE: Yeah. One of the pages that was removed in the past week is something called the Pedestrian Bicycle Crash Analysis Tool, which helps researchers and planners understand what happened in crashes involving cars and bikes and pedestrians so that they can prevent similar crashes. I talked to the researcher who originally led the development of this tool more than two decades ago. His name is David Harkey. Today he is the president of the Insurance Institute for Highway Safety, which is the big nonprofit that is funded by the auto insurance industry. Harkey is not happy that this tool is no longer available on the Federal Highway website.

sentences.en: ROSE: Yeah.
sentences.zh: 罗斯：是的。

sentences.en: One of the pages that was removed in the past week is something called the Pedestrian Bicycle Crash Analysis Tool, which helps researchers and planners understand what happened in crashes involving cars and bikes and pedestrians so that they can prevent similar crashes.
sentences.zh: 过去一周被删除的页面之一是“行人自行车碰撞分析工具”，它可以帮助研究人员和规划人员了解涉及汽车、自行车和行人的碰撞事故中发生的情况，以便防止类似的碰撞事故。

sentences.en: I talked to the researcher who originally led the development of this tool more than two decades ago.
sentences.zh: 我与二十多年前最初领导该工具开发的研究人员进行了交谈。

sentences.en: His name is David Harkey.
sentences.zh: 他的名字叫大卫·哈基。

sentences.en: Today he is the president of the Insurance Institute for Highway Safety, which is the big nonprofit that is funded by the auto insurance industry.
sentences.zh: 如今，他是公路安全保险协会的主席，该协会是由汽车保险行业资助的大型非营利组织。

sentences.en: Harkey is not happy that this tool is no longer available on the Federal Highway website.
sentences.zh: Harkey 很高兴联邦高速公路网站上不再提供此工具。

blocks.html_en: DAVID HARKEY: This is an agency that claims that safety is a priority for them, and safety involves everyone on the roadway. And so we're very disappointed in seeing these kinds of resources removed from the site.

sentences.en: DAVID HARKEY: This is an agency that claims that safety is a priority for them, and safety involves everyone on the roadway.
sentences.zh: 大卫·哈基：该机构声称安全是他们的首要任务，安全涉及道路上的每个人。

sentences.en: And so we're very disappointed in seeing these kinds of resources removed from the site.
sentences.zh: 因此，我们对此类资源从网站中删除感到非常失望。

blocks.html_en: ROSE: Harkey says his organization has been told that this particular tool is, quote, "under review." But he has no details about what the criteria of that review are or how long it might last.

sentences.en: ROSE: Harkey says his organization has been told that this particular tool is, quote, "under review."
sentences.zh: 罗斯：哈基说，他的组织已被告知，这个特定的工具正在“接受审查”。

sentences.en: But he has no details about what the criteria of that review are or how long it might last.
sentences.zh: 但他没有详细说明审查的标准是什么或可能持续多长时间。

blocks.html_en: MARTIN: Have we had any explanation of why this is happening?

sentences.en: MARTIN: Have we had any explanation of why this is happening?
sentences.zh: 马丁：我们对为什么会发生这种情况有任何解释吗？

blocks.html_en: ROSE: There has been no public explanation either from the Federal Highway Administration or from the Department of Transportation. But we did get an emailed statement from DOT yesterday. A spokesperson says the department continuously reviews its policies to ensure that they promote, quote, "safety, efficiency and common sense," unquote. The statement notes that nearly 80% of the U.S. population uses cars to get to work and says DOT is taking a closer look at federal guidance to ensure that American families have the infrastructure they need.

sentences.en: ROSE: There has been no public explanation either from the Federal Highway Administration or from the Department of Transportation.
sentences.zh: 罗斯：联邦公路管理局或交通部都没有公开解释。

sentences.en: But we did get an emailed statement from DOT yesterday.
sentences.zh: 但昨天我们确实收到了交通部发来的电子邮件声明。

sentences.en: A spokesperson says the department continuously reviews its policies to ensure that they promote, quote, "safety, efficiency and common sense," unquote.
sentences.zh: 一位发言人表示，该部门不断审查其政策，以确保它们提倡“安全、效率和常识”，而不是引用。

sentences.en: The statement notes that nearly 80% of the U.S. population uses cars to get to work and says DOT is taking a closer look at federal guidance to ensure that American families have the infrastructure they need.
sentences.zh: 声明指出，近 80% 的美国人口使用汽车上班，并表示交通部正在仔细研究联邦指导意见，以确保美国家庭拥有所需的基础设施。

blocks.html_en: The spokesperson also touted recent declines in the number of people killed on U.S. roads, which fell to about 36,000 last year. And it is true that the crash fatality rate in the U.S. is near an all-time low. But it still remains far higher than the crash rates in other developed countries - in some cases, double. And nearly 20% of people killed on U.S. roads last year - more than 6,700 - were pedestrians or bicyclists.

sentences.en: The spokesperson also touted recent declines in the number of people killed on U.S. roads, which fell to about 36,000 last year.
sentences.zh: 该发言人还表示，最近美国道路上死亡人数有所下降，去年降至约 36,000 人。

sentences.en: And it is true that the crash fatality rate in the U.S. is near an all-time low.
sentences.zh: 确实，美国的车祸死亡率接近历史最低点。

sentences.en: But it still remains far higher than the crash rates in other developed countries - in some cases, double.
sentences.zh: 但它仍然远远高于其他发达国家的事故率——在某些情况下，甚至是两倍。

sentences.en: And nearly 20% of people killed on U.S. roads last year - more than 6,700 - were pedestrians or bicyclists.
sentences.zh: 去年在美国道路上死亡的人中有近 20%（超过 6,700 人）是行人或骑自行车的人。

blocks.html_en: MARTIN: That is NPR's Joel Rose. Joel, thank you.

sentences.en: MARTIN: That is NPR's Joel Rose.
sentences.zh: 马丁：那是 NPR 的乔尔·罗斯。

sentences.en: Joel, thank you.
sentences.zh: 乔尔，谢谢你。

blocks.html_en: ROSE: You're welcome.

sentences.en: ROSE: You're welcome.
sentences.zh: 罗斯：不客气。

sentences.zh: （音乐原声片段）

content_html_zh:
华盛顿——自从交通部长肖恩·达菲 (Sean Duffy) 发誓要终止对“DEI 自行车道”的支持以来的两周内，越来越多的与自行车相关的安全材料从联邦公路管理局的网站上消失了。

交通部各机构网络支持部门的一位人士表示，就连机构工作人员也在猜测其中的原因。 他们要求匿名，因为他们担心公开发言会遭到报复。

该人士表示，联邦公路局工作人员已被要求查找并在某些情况下删除任何提及自行车和自行车道的内容。

相关报道：NPR

“为什么你需要取消自行车安全？” 他们告诉美国国家公共广播电台。

他们说：“你知道，我们并没有让网站变得更好、更快或更快，从而能够向使用这些资源的社区提供真实信息。” “真是愚蠢。”

特朗普政府对安全资源的审查仍存疑问

根据 Internet Archive Wayback Machine 的分析和 NPR 获得的 FHWA 内部电子邮件，联邦交通官员在过去一周撤下或编辑了数十页安全资源。

虽然没有官方解释，但这些变化似乎是从 FHWA 网站上查找和删除道路安全材料的更广泛努力的一部分。

本月早些时候，联邦公路管理局悄悄从“经过验证的安全对策”清单中删除了一系列建议，包括自行车道和测速摄像头，这些措施已经过机构工作人员的审查，并被证明可以改善道路安全。

交通部发言人周三在一份电子邮件声明中告诉 NPR，该部门“不断审查其政策，以确保每项政策都促进安全、效率和常识。”

该发言人表示：“由于近 80% 的人口使用汽车上班和进行重要的医疗预约，我们正在仔细研究联邦指导意见，以确保美国家庭拥有他们所需的基础设施。” “尽管左派认为每个人都应该骑自行车到鞋盒大小的公寓去吃蟋蟀，以实现虚假的气候议程，但这是一种常识性的做法。”

但安全倡导者警告说，特朗普政府正在破坏业经证实的减少道路交通事故和死亡人数的措施，其中包括一些可以追溯到几十年前的措施。

已被拆除的安全工具之一被称为行人和自行车碰撞分析工具（PBCAT）。 截至周四，FHWA 网站上以前托管该工具的页面链接已失效。

该工具旨在捕获涉及汽车、自行车和行人的碰撞事故的详细信息，包括车辆操纵和道路条件的组合导致碰撞事故，这可以帮助规划者和安全倡导者制定对策以防止类似碰撞事故。

北卡罗来纳大学公路安全研究中心最初开发该工具的项目首席研究员戴维·哈基 (David Harkey) 表示：“这非常重要，因为它可以帮助我们更好地追踪全国范围内的行人和骑自行车者的死亡情况。”

如今，哈基是公路安全保险协会 (IIHS) 的主席，这是一个由汽车保险行业资助的非营利组织，致力于减少机动车碰撞伤亡。

哈基告诉 NPR，他对从 FHWA 网站上删除该工具的决定感到失望。

“这个机构声称安全是他们的首要任务，”他说。 “重要的是，他们的网站上有像 PBCAT 这样经过验证的工具和资源，可供全国各地的安全专业人员使用。”

哈基说，即使 PBCAT 未在 FHWA 网站上公开提供，地方和州官员仍可能能够使用它，因为它已经嵌入到他们跟踪事故的方式中。 尽管如此，他还是希望 FHWA 能够广泛提供该工具。

根据他的组织与 FHWA 工作人员的对话，哈基表示该网络工具仍在“审查中”，但他表示细节尚不清楚。

哈基说：“我们不清楚正在审查什么、标准是什么、他们在寻找什么。” “希望这次审查能够早日完成。”

但哈基并不乐观。 他说，FHWA 在 18 个月前撤下了另一个重要的安全工具——一个名为高速公路安全信息系统的数据库。 它还没有恢复。

“这是这个过程中最可怕的事情之一，”哈基说。 “我们听到了同样的故事，事情正在接受审查，一旦审查完成，他们希望能够将其恢复。 但我们还没有看到这一点。”

达菲发布“DEI”帖子两天后，交通部工作人员被告知删除自行车安全用语

美国联邦公路管理局和交通运输部都没有回答有关审查标准或需要多长时间的问题。

但 NPR 看到的一封 FHWA 内部电子邮件提供了更清晰的信息，显示 FWHA 网站何时开始删除与自行车和安全相关的材料。

本月早些时候，交通部宣布提供超过 17 亿美元的酌情拨款，其中不包括自行车道或行人项目的资金。 相比之下，拜登政府利用同一计划为全国自行车道和小径以及其他道路和基础设施项目提供了数亿美元的资金。

时任交通部长皮特·布蒂吉格 (Pete Buttigieg) 在 2021 年宣布拨款时表示：“我们很自豪能够支持这些伟大的项目，这些项目将改善基础设施、加强供应链、让我们更安全、促进公平并应对气候变化。”

国务卿达菲在 7 月 7 日的 X 帖子中表示，拜登政府“将你们的钱用于 DEI 自行车道和气候变化。 结束了。”

对此，批评者指出，联邦公路管理局此前曾承认，自行车道可以使道路对所有用户来说更安全。

但 7 月 9 日，FHWA 安全办公室的一名工作人员发送了一封主题为“紧急行动：更新网站”的电子邮件，指示工作人员从网站上删除该语言。

具体来说，这封电子邮件指示工作人员对“经过验证的安全对策”列表进行重大更改，正如他们所知，这些措施已被证明可以减少事故并挽救生命。 发件人写道：“我们收到了领导层的紧急要求，要求根据管理方向更新网络内容。”他要求将自行车道、测速摄像头和其他与速度相关的策略从列表中删除。

安全倡导者表示，资助优先事项从一届政府转移到另一届政府是正常的。 但许多人表示，他们对交通部网站上取消基于证据的安全措施的推动感到不安。

相关报道：NPR

相关报道：NPR

为 DOT 机构提供网络支持的人员也有同样的担忧。

“好的，明白了。 不再有补助金，”他们说。 “但这为什么意味着我需要删除内容呢？ 比如，步行自行车碰撞分析工具——它伤害了谁，你知道吗？”

去年，超过 36,000 人在美国道路上丧生。 自 2021 年以来，这一数字一直呈下降趋势，美国的车祸死亡率接近历史最低点。 尽管如此，这里的车祸率和死亡率仍然远高于其他发达国家。

到 2025 年，死亡总数包括超过 6,700 名行人和骑自行车的人。

“安全涉及道路上的每个人，”IIHS 主席哈基说。 “因此，作为一个机构，如果你真的相信你的工作是确保安全是首要任务，那么你就无法移除可以帮助你解决道路上 20% 死亡事故的工具和资源。”

成绩单

米歇尔·马丁，主持人：

特朗普的交通部正试图对自行车道实施刹车。 本月早些时候，交通部长肖恩·达菲 (Sean Duffy) 攻击了他所说的——我在这里引用——“DEI 自行车道”。 现在，交通官员正在从联邦网站上删除提及自行车的安全材料。 NPR 的乔尔·罗斯今天对此进行了新报道，他现在就在我们身边。 早上好，乔尔。

乔尔·罗斯连线：嘿，米歇尔。

马丁：好的。 我们可以从 - 什么是 DEI 自行车道开始吗？

罗斯：这是运输部长肖恩·达菲本月早些时候在社交媒体帖子中使用的一句话。 达菲宣布提供 17 亿美元的联邦拨款，其中不包括用于自行车或步行项目的任何资金。 达菲试图与拜登政府形成鲜明对比，拜登政府利用相同的拨款计划为自行车道和休闲步道提供了数亿美元的资金，并宣扬这些项目（包括自行车道）的股权效益。

一些特朗普政府的批评者随后指出，隶属于交通部的联邦公路管理局此前曾承认，自行车道使道路对每个人来说都更安全。 第二天，联邦公路管理局的网络支持人员收到了一项紧急请求，要求从网站上删除该语言，以符合白宫政策。

马丁：我们确切知道什么被删除了吗？

罗斯：是的。 大约两周前，交通部削减了一系列已被证明可以减少事故并挽救生命的安全措施。 其中五项建议已悄悄从列表中删除，包括自行车道和超速摄像头。 我们本周了解到其他安全材料也消失了。 我与一位为 DOT 各机构从事网络支持工作的人进行了交谈。 他们要求匿名，因为他们担心公开发言会遭到报复。 他们说，联邦公路管理局的工作人员已被指示在在线安全材料中查找“自行车”和“自行车道”一词。 在过去的一周里，工作人员编辑甚至撤下了数十页内容。

马丁：你能给我们举个例子吗？

罗斯：是的。 过去一周被删除的页面之一是“行人自行车碰撞分析工具”，它可以帮助研究人员和规划人员了解涉及汽车、自行车和行人的碰撞事故中发生的情况，以便防止类似的碰撞事故。 我与二十多年前最初领导该工具开发的研究人员进行了交谈。 他的名字叫大卫·哈基。 如今，他是公路安全保险协会的主席，该协会是由汽车保险行业资助的大型非营利组织。 Harkey 很高兴联邦高速公路网站上不再提供此工具。

大卫·哈基：该机构声称安全是他们的首要任务，安全涉及道路上的每个人。 因此，我们对此类资源从网站中删除感到非常失望。

罗斯：哈基说，他的组织已被告知，这个特定的工具正在“接受审查”。 但他没有详细说明审查的标准是什么或可能持续多长时间。

马丁：我们对为什么会发生这种情况有任何解释吗？

罗斯：联邦公路管理局或交通部都没有公开解释。 但昨天我们确实收到了交通部发来的电子邮件声明。 一位发言人表示，该部门不断审查其政策，以确保它们提倡“安全、效率和常识”，而不是引用。 声明指出，近 80% 的美国人口使用汽车上班，并表示交通部正在仔细研究联邦指导意见，以确保美国家庭拥有所需的基础设施。

该发言人还表示，最近美国道路上死亡人数有所下降，去年降至约 36,000 人。 确实，美国的车祸死亡率接近历史最低点。 但它仍然远远高于其他发达国家的事故率——在某些情况下，甚至是两倍。 去年在美国道路上死亡的人中有近 20%（超过 6,700 人）是行人或骑自行车的人。

马丁：那是 NPR 的乔尔·罗斯。 乔尔，谢谢你。

罗斯：不客气。

（音乐原声片段）
